title: grewia celtidifolia
intro: Grewia celtidifolia, also known as the False Mahua, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical regions of India, Southeast Asia, and the Pacific Islands. This tree has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Grewia celtidifolia include its ability to treat fever, digestive issues, and skin problems, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Grewia celtidifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat various ailments, and its bark and roots can also be used to make medicinal tinctures and ointments.
